pattern: After sorting the three positions, the two gaps between neighbors decide everything: the maximum is just the empty slots to fill, while the minimum collapses to a small case analysis of those gaps.
hints: Sort the positions so they are x < y < z; only the two gaps (y - x) and (z - y) matter.
The maximum number of moves slides endpoints inward one slot at a time, which equals the total number of empty interior positions.

For the minimum, if the stones are already consecutive it is 0; if either gap is exactly 2 (or one stone is adjacent leaving a single hole) a single move finishes it; otherwise two moves bring both endpoints next to the middle.

edge_inputs.description: one big gap allows one or two moves
unsorted input with a single-hole gap
exactly one empty slot between two of them forces minimum 1
consecutive but given out of order
gap of two on one side only

stress.note: sample guarantees three different values as required
